VAN DER BEEK TIMMO
timmo.vanderbeek@laboratoirelaser.com
B11, ELghazala Technopark
Activité Nous développons des solutions photoniques 
pour l'agriculture. Notre produit principal est un outil 
de gestion d'irrigation pour prévention de sous ou sur 
irrigation. Par un capteur imagerie laser, nous suivons le 
besoin d'eau dans un champs en temps réel. Outre cela, le 
capteur signale la santé générale des plantes.
Aspect Technologique Nous utilisons la lumière et l'imagerie. 
La stabilisation du capteur et l'alignement des faisceaux 
sont les principaux défis techniques.
Originalité Nous mesurons une moyenne dans un champs 
entier en temps réel au lieu de mesurer localement.
Activity We develop photonic solutions for agriculture. 
Our main product is a irrigation management tool for 
over- and underirrigation. Using a laser imaging sensor, 
we capture the water need of all the plants in a field in 
realtime. The sensor also signals the general health of 
plants.
Technological Aspect We use light sources and imaging. 
Sensor stabilization and laser alignment are the main 
technological challenges. 
Originality We measure an entire field and we take an 
average in real time instead of a local measurement.
FR
EN

Tunisia · Precision irrigation
# Your field tells you _when to water_
LDIMS listens to your plants — not the weather forecast. The first irrigation management system driven by real-time plant stress response.
Request a demo kit Become a distributor
Field-tested in Tunisia
Partners for Water programme
White-label available
Field trial · Medjez el Bab, Tunisia · 2025
What makes LLA different
We don’t just publish research — we build tools that work outside the lab. Every project at Laboratoire Laser Afrique is designed from the start to be deployable, affordable, and adapted to the realities of agriculture and industry in Africa and beyond.
How it works
## Plant intelligence, field-ready
Four steps from sensor to irrigation decision.
01
### Sensors in the field
Compact sensor masts deployed across your plots continuously capture plant physiological signals — no soil probes, no sampling delays.
02
### Real-time stress analysis
The ensemble response of the crop to drought and other stress factors is computed in real time and translated into actionable irrigation indices.
03
### Farmer dashboard
A simple mobile and web dashboard answers three questions: when to irrigate, how long, and how much. No agronomy degree required.
04
### Connects to your setup
Works alongside any existing drip or sprinkler system. No need to replace infrastructure — LDIMS adds intelligence to what you already have.
LDIMS vs conventional systems
Conventional approach
Soil sensors — measure environment, not the crop
Weather-based schedules — ignore actual plant state
Fixed irrigation cycles — risk over or under-watering
Reactive — stress noticed days after onset
LDIMS
Plant sensors — the crop signals directly
Real-time stress index — based on actual crop physiology
Data-driven decisions — right amount, right time
Proactive — stress detected at earliest onset
Partners for Water · Tunisia · 2025
From the field
## Tested where it matters
Active LDIMS trials in Medjez el Bab, in collaboration with the Partners for Water programme. Results being published — contact us for a briefing.
2025
Active LDIMS field trial, Medjez el Bab
Real-time
Plant stress data streamed continuously
Live
Results in progress — ask for a briefing
![LDIMS sensor mast in Medjez el Bab field trial, Tunisia 2025](https://laboratoirelaser.com/)
Field trial 2025
### Medjez el Bab, Tunisia
LDIMS sensor masts deployed across drip-irrigated plots. Collective crop stress response measured continuously in real field conditions, in collaboration with the Partners for Water programme.
“This is not another weather station. LDIMS reads the crop directly — a fundamentally different approach to knowing when your field needs water.”
Field trial team · Laboratoire Laser Afrique · 2025
Who we work with
## Built to distribute
Whether you resell technology, run a farm, or supply agricultural inputs — our systems are designed to integrate into your business.
### Tech resellers
White-label hardware and software bundle. Margin-friendly pricing, full technical documentation and training.
### Nurseries & farms
Deploy on your own land first. Subscription dashboard, no server management. Financing available for pilots.
### Agricultural suppliers
Bundle our products with your existing lines. Co-branded packaging. Volume discounts from 10 units.
Partnership process
1
### Request a demo kit
We ship a unit to you. Test in real conditions — no commitment required.
2
### Choose your model
Reseller, white-label, or OEM. We adapt pricing and branding to your business.
3
### We support your rollout
Installation guides, remote support, staff training and partner portal.
4
### Grow together
Co-marketing, joint case studies, and product development based on your clients’ feedback.
Also from our lab
## Two more technologies in development
Explore our other projects
Beyond LDIMS, Laboratoire Laser Afrique is developing solutions for humanitarian energy access and preventive plant health monitoring.
Solar energy
### Solar Oven for Yemen
A lightweight, affordable solar-heated bread oven designed for humanitarian deployment. Using innovative reflective materials, the oven mantle reaches 200°C within minutes — even in winter — without fuel or electricity.
Solar thermalLightweight materials200°C in minutesOff-grid
Plant diagnostics
### PlanOptic Monitor
A preventive plant pathology monitoring system using laser imaging to detect disease and stress in crops before visible symptoms appear. Early detection means less pesticide, less loss, and better yields.
Laser imagingEarly detectionPreventive monitoringPrecision agri
